column14: Just a bit cumbersome but I cannot think of a better way to do it anyhow
column26: Need some getting used to before it's second nature, but overall pretty intuitive nonetheless

column14: My ratings here are lower than prior task's because one, it was generally harder to "grasp" everything quickly. Two, I thought the labeling was not very straightforward, especially as it pertained to complex expressions. Finally, one other point: I don't think the documentation helped me a lot with "big picture" idea for for loops and making labels and steps dynamic. For instance, after reading the documentation, I don't know how to merge user provided input with steps.
column26: Expressions were especially tough to "get" as far as labels and dynamic input goes. The way I look at if I "get it" is if I remember or recall with ease the way of implementation, which for quite a few subtopics for Task 2, I don't. Hope this helps!

column14: Getting the formula needed me to think carefully about assignment, labelling was straight forward
column26: The labels were straightforward like others, but the creation of  step inside the for loop needed me to pick values according to index and make sure it is rendered correctly

column14: Getting the formula walkthrough working the way I wanted: This was the least intuitive because what I was originally thinking was to replace the values into the formula itself rather than adding labels, and it took me a while to understand I should just be writing labels.
column26: It was unintuitive but technically it was easy once I understood it.

column14: All of the steps were fine.
column26: It was also easy to implement -> once the template of the formula was setup, writing the steps was fast.

column14: For me, the implementation of the steps was a bit unintuitive, and it required having a lot of the exact values correct and in place.
column26: For the expected value formula you needed an array of values, which was difficult to edit because you couldn't edit each individual value. It was very hard to increase the number of values or individually edit them or the probabilities, and labeling was a bit tricky because I wanted to display both the current probability and the whole array, which I couldn't do.

column14: Creating steps and adding descriptions/labels is very simple, though some elements of the library are not quite yet covered yet (e.g. allowing subsets of arrays to be draggable).
column26: Adding steps follows the same config syntax style which makes it easy to understand how to add them.

column14: For Labeling a variable (text and value), I did not intially think it was possible from my reading of the tutorial, but after trying it out, it does work.
column26: Initially I was a bit stuck on how to represent different steps of a summation, and I was pointed towards the ability to define arrays in variables and was able to figure it out.

column14: I think it's pretty intuitive but the semantics get's really cluttered and a little confusing when you want to represent different things
column26: I think I was confused at some points on whether I should put something as a description or as a label

column14: sometimes difficult to figure out how to highlight a specfic part
